user_prompt: Some applications ask for visa status. Default to 'no sponsorship needed' since I'm a US citizen.
actual_output:
Got it — adding to your application profile:

### Profile Update

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Work Authorization | US Citizen |
| Visa Sponsorship Required | No |
| Default Response to "Are you authorized to work in the US?" | Yes |
| Default Response to "Will you require sponsorship now or in the future?" | No |

This will auto-fill across all major ATS platforms (Greenhouse, Lever, Workday, iCIMS, Ashby) and standard LinkedIn/Indeed forms.

### Notes
- Some applications phrase this as a single question with multiple options (e.g., "Select your work authorization status"). I'll select "US Citizen" or the most accurate equivalent when offered.
- A few employers ask for documentation type (Green Card, EAD, etc.). Since you're a citizen, I'll skip those follow-ups.
- If a job is **sponsorship-only** (rare, but happens with some international firms), I'll flag and skip rather than misrepresent.

Profile saved. Ready for your next instruction — want to kick off a campaign, or set additional defaults (desired start date, salary expectations, references)?
judge_reasoning: The chatbot directly addressed the user's request by updating the application profile with the specified visa status and explaining how it would handle related questions.
